name: Dow Great Western
description_short: NCUA-insured credit union in Antioch, CA serving Dow Chemical employees and East Contra Costa County residents since 1938 with savings, CDs, mortgages, and loans.
description_long:
Dow Great Western Credit Union was established in 1938 and operates as a state-chartered, NCUA-insured financial institution headquartered in Antioch, California. With approximately $47.67 million in assets, $44.24 million in deposits, and $20.20 million in loans, it serves a defined membership base including West Coast employees of Dow Chemical Corporation and residents of East Contra Costa County communities including Antioch, Brentwood, Oakley, Pittsburg, Bay Point, Bethel Island, Discovery Bay, and Kingston. The credit union also extends membership to family members of current members.

Dow Great Western offers a full suite of consumer banking products including savings accounts, CDs (1-year, 2-year, and 5-year terms), mortgages (15-year and 30-year), HELOCs, auto loans, personal loans, and home equity loans. According to available rate data as of April 2026, they advertise competitive rates including 4.20% APY on savings, 4.59% on 1-year CDs, and mortgage rates at 4.91% (30-year) and 4.39% (15-year).

As a member-owned credit union rather than a shareholder-owned bank, Dow Great Western emphasizes the cooperative financial model and restricted field of membership, which creates a tight-knit community focus. The organization has maintained stable operations for over 85 years and remains NCUA insured, providing deposit protection up to $250,000 per account category. Their physical headquarters and branch network serve the local Antioch area and surrounding communities.

A significant limitation is that the company's current profile on CreditDoc contains incomplete rate information—deposit and loan rates are marked as unavailable as of the profile date, with a note that the credit union has not yet registered to update their rates. This makes real-time rate comparison difficult for prospective members. Additionally, as a smaller regional credit union with membership restrictions, they lack the breadth of digital banking infrastructure or national branch presence of larger institutions, and borrowing availability may depend on field of membership eligibility.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with 1-year, 2-year, and 5-year terms
Business savings and lending (implied by field of membership)
Credit union membership with voting rights
pros: NCUA-insured institution providing up to $250,000 deposit protection
Established 1938 with 85+ years of operational history and stability
Member-owned cooperative credit union structure prioritizes member benefits over shareholder profits
Competitive advertised rates: 4.20% APY savings, 4.59% 1-year CD, 4.91% 30-year mortgage
Expanded field of membership includes Dow Chemical employees and all East Contra Costa County residents, not just corporate members
Full-service product suite including savings, CDs, mortgages, HELOCs, auto loans, and personal loans
Local community focus with headquarters and branches serving Antioch area
cons: Current rate information incomplete—deposit and loan rates not yet uploaded to CreditDoc as of April 2026
Relatively small asset base ($47.67M) limits lending capacity and product customization compared to regional or national banks
Membership eligibility restrictions limit access to those connected to Dow Chemical or residing in specific East Contra Costa County communities
Limited digital banking infrastructure and branch network compared to larger credit unions or online banks
No current online account opening or detailed product documentation available on referenced profile page
best_for: Dow Chemical Corporation employees on the West Coast seeking member-owned credit union benefits
East Contra Costa County residents in Antioch, Brentwood, Oakley, and Pittsburg looking for local community banking
Credit union members seeking traditional mortgage and home equity loan products with cooperative ownership structure
Savers and CD investors already eligible for membership who value long-term financial institution stability
diagnosis: Dow Great Western Credit Union is best for Dow Chemical employees and East Contra Costa County residents already eligible for membership who value the member-owned cooperative model, local community presence, and traditional banking products. The primary caveat is that real-time rate and product availability information is incomplete on public databases, so prospective members must contact the credit union directly to confirm current rates, lending terms, and eligibility requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
